channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Venezuela quakes final death toll expected to be 'much higher'
item.description: Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes killed nearly 3,000, updated official figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors in the rubble of the disaster. FRANCE 24's Maxime Pluvinet reports from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Paris appeals court ruling could upend Marine Le Pen’s 2027 presidential bid
item.description: Paris' appeals court is set to rule Tuesday in Marine Le Pen’s embezzlement case, a decision that could determine whether one of France’s leading presidential contenders can run in next year’s election.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US celebrates 250 years of independence amid polarising political landscape
item.description: The 250th anniversary of American independence is colliding with a country gripped by political polarisation and a heat wave bearing down on millions of people across multiple states as celebrations got underway Saturday throughout the United States.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: The week in pictures: America at 250, Taylor Swift gets married and a 'massive attack' on Kyiv
item.description: America marks 250 years of independence under a heatwave, a massive Russian attack sets Kyiv ablaze, France dominates Sweden at the World Cup, and Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce get married at Madison Square Garden: FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Rescue teams wind down operations as death toll nears 3,000
item.description: Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes killed nearly 3,000, updated official figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors in the rubble of the disaster. Tens of thousands more are still reported missing.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Will the far-right leader be barred from the French presidency?
item.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen’s political fate will be decided on Tuesday when a Paris appeals court rules on whether she is eligible to run in the 2027 presidential elections, a verdict with major implications for France's future. Le Pen is appealing her 2025 conviction for embezzling European Parliament funds to pay employees in her party.
item.dc:creator: Romain BRUNET

item.title: Vingegaard takes yellow jersey as Tour de France begins in Barcelona
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard took an early lead in his quest for a third Tour de France title ​as the Visma-Lease a Bike rider recorded the fastest time in stage one – a 19.6-km team time trial in Barcelona on Saturday. Last year's runner-up Vingegaard, who won the race in 2022 and 2023, finished ​12 seconds ahead ‌of Slovenian rival Tadej Pogacar, who has won the Tour de France ⁠four times, including the last two editions.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France to face Morocco in quarter-final showdown
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scored from the penalty spot as France overcame searing heat and stubborn defence to beat Paraguay 1-0 and set up a World Cup quarter-final against Morocco on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France beat Paraguay to reach World Cup quarter-finals
item.description: Kylian Mbappe scored from the penalty spot as France overcame searing heat and stubborn defence to beat Paraguay 1-0 and set up a World Cup quarter-final against Morocco on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Les Bleus advance to World Cup quarter-finals after bruising win over Paraguay
item.description: Kylian Mbappé scored the only goal of the game from a second-half penalty as France beat Paraguay in a cagey Round of 16 tie in Philadelphia on Saturday. Les Bleus will take on Morocco in the quarter-finals on Thursday in Boston.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Live: Putin and Zelensky speak to Trump ahead of NATO summit in Turkey
item.description: US President Donald Trump spoke with Russia's Vladimir Putin and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelensky late Saturday to discuss the conflict in Ukraine ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara on July 7 and 8. Negotiations remain at an impasse, with Moscow demanding the withdrawal of Ukrainian forces from Ukraine's Donetsk region – a demand Kyiv rejects. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump again blasts 'communists', hails US as history's 'crowning achievement' on 250th anniversary
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Saturday hailed America as the "crowning achievement of human history" in a Fourth of July speech celebrating 250 years of US independence in Washington. Trump claimed that under his presidency, the United States was "prouder than ever before", even as he used the event to renew his attack on domestic opponents he branded "communists".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Les Bleus discuss refereeing after Paraguay game
item.description:
🇫🇷⚽️ Les Bleus secured their place in the World Cup 2026 quarter-finals after a very tense match against #Paraguay 🇵🇾, marked by numerous fouls that went unpunished.
Despite the intensity, the #French players reacted with composure after the final whistle, focusing on qualification rather than the controversy.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France-Paraguay : French fans in Philly react to 1-0 score
item.description:
🇫🇷🇵🇾 #France beat #Paraguay 1-0 on Saturday in a frustrating and tense Round of 16 clash, controlling large stretches of the game but needing a Kylian #Mbappé penalty to finally break through.
🎤 Here's what France fans in Philly had to say about the game.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé sends Les Bleus into quarterfinals after hard-fought win over Paraguay
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé sends Les Bleus into quarterfinals after hard-fought win over Paraguay
item.description: France edged past a stubborn and physical Paraguay side thanks to a penalty kick by Kylian Mbappé (1-0), sealing their place in the World Cup 2026 quarterfinals after a frustrating but dominant display. They will take on Morocco on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Hezbollah, Hamas and Houthi officials attend funeral ceremonies for Iran's Khamenei
item.description: Tens of thousands of mourners flocked to Tehran's Grand Mosalla Mosque to pay their respects to late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday. Among them were representatives from Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis of Yemen, members of the "axis of resistance" that Iran has long supported as part of its years-long struggle against Israel.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000 as tens of thousands still reported missing
item.description: Fatalities from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes jumped to nearly 3,000 people, official updated figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors. Tens of thousands more are still reported missing following the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude shocks on June 24.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Paraguay–France: Mbappé penalty puts Les Bleus ahead in tense Round of 16 clash
item.description: France edged past Paraguay 1-0 in the Round of 16 of the World Cup 2026, maintaining their perfect tournament record and moving one step closer to another global title. Les Bleus’ dominant group stage performances have established them as genuine contenders, while Paraguay’s resistance was eventually broken by a Kylian Mbappé penalty after a tense and physical contest. Relive our liveblog for minute-to-minute commentary.
item.dc:creator: Mehdi BOUZOUINA

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ounahi fires Morocco to quarter-finals with decisive brace
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ounahi fires Morocco to quarter-finals with decisive brace
item.description: Morocco secured their passage to the quarter-finals with a 3-0 victory over Canada in Houston, with Selim Ounahi's double proving decisive. The Lions of the Atlas overcame a difficult first half to eventually overwhelm their North American hosts and progress as one of the tournament's standout sides.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Armenia’s defiant drag scene braves pushback
item.description:
Armenia sits at the crossroads of Europe, Asia and the Middle East. While in much of the European Union, the drag scene is mainstream, in Armenia it remains underground. Performers face threats of violence, stigma, police pressure and even rejection by their own families.
Despite this, drag queens have built a vibrant, bustling community that has attracted both local Armenians and international crowds. They make up a passionate and determined microculture challenging ultraconservative norms.
And this avant garde scene is, in turn, being influenced by Armenia’s evolving political landscape.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Vingegaard takes early Tour de France lead as Visma win opening stage in Barcelona
item.description: Danish cyclist Jonas Vingegaard's Visma-Lease a Bike outfit came first in the opening team time-trial of this year's Tour de France in Barcelona on Saturday. Slovenian rival Tadej Pogacar, who put in a strong performance, is hoping to become the fifth cyclist to win the Tour de France five times.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Curtis Young on America's "diffcult moment in the mirror"
item.description: This weekend, the United States marks 250 years since the adoption of the Declaration of Independence, one of the most influential political texts in modern history. Its assertion that governments derive their power from the consent of the governed, and that all people are endowed with the rights to life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness, has inspired democratic movements around the world.
item.dc:creator: Gavin LEE
